prerequisites: A Microsoft SQL Server instance reachable by Airbyte: Azure SQL Database or SQL Server 2016 or greater.
Network access from Airbyte to the database. For databases behind a VPC or firewall, allowlist Airbyte's IP addresses.
The target database (set in the `database` field) and the target `schema` (commonly `dbo`).
A SQL Server user (`user`) with ALTER permissions on the target schema so Airbyte can create and modify tables, plus the ability to create tables and write rows.
To use Bulk load: an Azure Blob Storage account and container, a Shared Access Signature (SAS) token, and an external data source configured in MSSQL for the BULK INSERT command.

authMethods.name: SQL Server username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate the JDBC connection with a database `user` and `password`. The user must hold ALTER permissions on the target schema and be able to create tables and write rows.

authMethods.name: Bulk load via Azure Blob Storage SAS token
authMethods.summary: When `load_type` is set to Bulk, Airbyte stages data in Azure Blob Storage and runs BULK INSERT. Authenticate to the storage container with a Shared Access Signature (the `load_type.shared_access_signature` secret).

authMethods.name: Encrypted SSL with trust store password
authMethods.summary: When `ssl_method` uses encrypted verify-certificate mode, provide the trust store password (`ssl_method.trustStorePassword`) so the connector can validate the server's certificate.

setupSteps.title: Prepare the SQL Server database and user
setupSteps.body: Ensure the SQL Server instance (Azure SQL Database or SQL Server 2016+) is reachable from Airbyte. Create or identify the target `database` and `schema` (e.g. `dbo`), and create a `user` with ALTER permissions on the target schema and rights to create tables and write rows.

setupSteps.title: Enter connection details
setupSteps.body: Set `host`, `port`, `database`, and `schema`. Provide the `user` and its `password`. Optionally set `jdbc_url_params` to pass extra JDBC connection properties as `key=value` pairs joined with `&`.

setupSteps.title: Choose an SSL method
setupSteps.body: Set `ssl_method` to one of: unencrypted; encrypted with trust server certificate (skips verification, for testing only); or encrypted with verify certificate. For the verify-certificate option, supply `ssl_method.trustStorePassword` to validate the server certificate.

setupSteps.title: Choose a load type
setupSteps.body: Set `load_type` to Insert to load data with SQL INSERT statements, or to Bulk to stage data in Azure Blob Storage and load it with BULK INSERT. For Bulk, provide the Azure storage account name, container name, the BULK load external data source name, and the `load_type.shared_access_signature` (SAS) token.

setupSteps.title: Test the connection
setupSteps.body: Save the destination and run Airbyte's connection test to confirm the credentials, network access, schema permissions, and (for Bulk) the Azure Blob Storage access are valid.

sources.title: MS SQL Server Destination Connector - Airbyte Documentation
